# The ampoule, computed (regenerated by sealed_unit.py)

## The inventory ledger (deposited power per GBq)

| isotope | half life (y) | emission | deposited power per GBq |
|---|---|---|---|
| 3H | 12.3 | beta, 5.7 keV avg | 0.9 µW |
| 63Ni | 100 | beta, 17 keV avg | 2.8 µW |
| 90Sr/90Y | 28.8 | beta pair, 1.13 MeV | 181.0 µW |
| 238Pu | 87.7 | alpha, 5.59 MeV | 895.6 µW |
| 241Am | 432 | alpha 5.49 + gamma 0.06 MeV | 889.2 µW |

## The lamp mismatch, step by step

- VUV photon production: 1 GBq x 5.5 MeV x 30% / 8.36 eV = **2.0e+14 photons/s**
- lamp bandwidth: 10 nm at 148 nm = 1.4e+14 Hz; spectral flux = **1.45 photons/cm2/s/Hz**
- nuclear integrated cross section (lambda^2/2pi) x Gamma_rad = 3.50e-11 cm2 x 6.3e-05 Hz = **2.2e-15 cm2 Hz**
- excitation rate per nucleus R = 3.2e-15 /s; per voxel of 1e8 nuclei = 3.2e-07 /s, i.e. one write quantum per **36 days**
- verdict: the bandwidth mismatch (lamp 1e+14 Hz / line 6e-05 Hz = 2e+18) cannot be bought back with activity. Broadband self pumping of neV lines is dead; the sealed unit computes in rates, not isomer populations.

## Boundary bandwidth (detected budget 1e7 events/s)

| precision b (bits) | counts per read 2^2b | reads per second |
|---|---|---|
| 4 | 256 | 39,062 |
| 6 | 4,096 | 2,441 |
| 8 | 65,536 | 153 |
| 10 | 1,048,576 | 10 |
| 12 | 16,777,216 | 1 |

## The worked 1 GBq 90Sr ampoule

- proposal budget 1e+06/s across 64 sites at 26 decays per independent sample (measured, /simulator): **38,462 samples/s at birth**
  - year 0: 100% of birth throughput, identical stationary law (aging theorem)
  - year 29: 50% of birth throughput, identical stationary law (aging theorem)
  - year 58: 25% of birth throughput, identical stationary law (aging theorem)
  - year 86: 12% of birth throughput, identical stationary law (aging theorem)
